entry.title: 🇮🇳 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #2 (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
REGISTRATION FOR THIS EVENT:  We will send confirmation emails two weeks before the event. You can still register, and you will be added to a waitlist if the maximum capacity is reached. 

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Join us for this 6-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Hyderabad, India.

This is the second of two CodeJams that we will do on the event-driven integration topic in Hyderabad.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one in the diagram below by the end of the CodeJam.

If you are interested in knowing what we will develop during the event, check out the Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will go through during the event.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Tuesday, July 16th 2025, in Infosys STP, Infosys Rd, Madhava Reddy Colony, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500032, India. The event is kindly being hosted by Infosys, thank you!

The instructors for this event are Developer Advocate @ajay_soreng and @ajmaradiaga 

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page.

Location: Infosys STP, Infosys Rd, Madhava Reddy Colony, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500032, India

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP Developers!

I've got some exciting news to share with you. We are organising an SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India!

I understand that in July, it is still season for the king of fruits in India (🥭 mango, obviously), and my colleague @ajay_soreng and I ( @ajmaradiaga ) will go on the hunt for the best mango variety in India... while we do a few CodeJams on our roadshow 😋.  We will have three stops on the roadshow from July 14th to 18th, 2025, doing a total of four CodeJams, one in Mumbai, two in Hyderabad, and one more in Bengaluru :-).

 

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape.

Event-driven integration with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about event-driven architectures focusing on SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. During the CodeJam, we will cover different technologies/tools: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP Business Application Studio, CloudEvents, and Node.js.

entry.title: [Cutover] Purchase Order data cleansing/migration - scope extract
entry.content:
This blog is to share some personal insights about Purchase Order data cleansing from enterprise perspective when the project is ongoing for cutover.

This approach is available for below data migration scenarios:

1) ECC upgrade to S/4 HANA (system conversion project)

2) Selective S/4 HANA client to client (selective data transition project)

To understand the context, we follow below analysis sequence:

1. Concept of Open PO & PO with history

2.Purchase Order Data Migration Scenario for Project Cutover

3. PO scenario in legacy SAP

4. Use T-code MB5S to analyze GR/IR quantity compared with total quantity

Here we download spreadsheet if data volume is not huge. If data volume is huge, here we should consider ETL tool -BODS for further analysis.

In spreadsheet, we analyze 8 cases following case rules (above slides) to calculate migrated quantity:

case 1: column H formula =IF(AND((D2=0),(F2=0)),"YES","NO")

case 2: column J formula =IF(AND((D2=0),(F2=C2)),"YES","NO")

case 3: column L formula =IF(AND((D2=F2),(D2<C2),(D2<>0)),"YES","NO")

case 4: column N formula =IF(AND((D2<F2),(F2=C2),(D2<>0)),"YES","NO")

case 5: column N formula =IF(AND((D2=C2),(D2>F2),(F2=0)),"YES","NO")

case 6: column R formula =IF(AND((D2=C2),(D2>F2),(F2<>0)),"YES","NO")

case 7: column T formula =IF(AND((C2>D2),(D2>F2),(F2<>0)),"YES","NO")

case 8: column X formula =IF(AND((C2>D2),(D2>F2),(F2=0)),"YES","NO")

based on corresponding case, we calculate migrated quantity on column I,K,M,O,S,U,V,X,Y.

Then we copy it to new sheet and delete the analysis case, only remain the migrated quantity.

we calculate the final migration quantity on column S, making different columns into one column, using excel "Filter" formula: =FILTER(H2:Q2,H2:Q2<>"") to ignore the blank value calculation in each line.

After analysis, the output result is what we need of the legacy PO number and item scope for migration.

Next step is to extract other legacy fields based on scoped PO to map with target system S/4 HANA fields.

Here for extraction tool, we have two tool solutions considering by data volume, because Excel sheet only can filter 18,000 lines:

1)data volume is small, less than 15,000, using SQVI.

2)data volume is huge, more than 15,000, using BODS.

5. SQVI approach

T-code: SQVI

As we know, Legacy PO may be created by referring:

1)Cost center

2)Purchase contract

3)PS-WBS

4)GL account

5)Purchase Info record

6)Purchase request

7) Internal Order

In PO account assignment - table EKKN, we should pay attention to cost center/GL account/WBS/internal order value.

As PO contract/PIR/PR existed in PO line item, it depends on project data object scope. Normally it will be migrated after post go-live or not considering for migration, because it will not impact finance initial balance.

In general, once we get the data scope (data key) in legacy, it is easy to extract any field we need, data scope is the first essential step for data migration activity.

Finally hope this blog will help you understand how to identify data scope for PO data migration or cutover activity.

entry.title: Re: How to update the 3E Regulation Content in EHS？
entry.content:
Dear all

i am sorry. One topoc is as well important to understand. 3E does have (best knowledge) two variants to "manage" the content (here the legal data)

Depending on the 3E license you have you can only use one of them. It is well know that the "content" (legal data) is different between the solutions. 

C.B

PS: but whatever licese you have; if you have prepared a "cookbook" which explains the steps.. then you can just go on.

The "more" important part is: if there is new data: what is now the "business impact". And this topci is no managed very well by using "Cookbooks"

For Reference only: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-members/about-content-providing-a-challenge-for-provider-and-company-who-need/ba-p/13279612

As any company (i know) use a slightly different process in using the 3E content: no futher help is possible

entry.title: Re: Technical Clearing Account in New Asset Accounting
entry.content:
Hi Shiva,

As you are already aware about how Technical clearing account functionality works in SAP and wants to know practical example and reason for this, let me give you a practical example.

Lets say Company is having a 2 Accounting principles - Local GAAP and IFRS. 

Asset Acquisition postings - System will post split accounting into Operation and Valuation part.

Operation part -

  • Vendor Account CR ( All Ledgers - Accounting principles )
  • Technical Clearing Account DR ( All Ledgers - Accounting principles )

Now comes the second party i.e. Valuation part. System will post accounting document for each accounting principle. So 2 accounting documents will be posted. 

First accounting document of valuation part :

  • Asset Account DR ( Local GAAP )
  • Technical Clearing CR ( Local GAAP )

Second accounting document of valuation part :

  • Asset Account DR ( IFRS )
  • Technical Clearing CR ( IFRS )

Coming to your question, why this split of operation and valuation part and logic behind this. 

As each accounting principle will have different rules on accounting. This will help to post document accordingly to accounting principle.

So take an example - Asset Cost - 100 & Freight Cost -10

IFRS might consider Total Asset cost as 110 but Local GAAP will consider Asset cost as 100 and Freight cost as an expense. Also we have a separate depreciation area for Accounting principles.

 

I hope this will help you to understand the concept.

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Delivery 80000057 does not exist in the database or i...
entry.content:
Delivery number 80000057 does not exist in the database.
Please check the maximum existing delivery number in table LIKP using transaction SE16N.

  • If the current number (80000057) exceeds the highest number found in the table, it indicates that the number range interval may not be set correctly or has reached its maximum limit.

  • In that case, update the number range interval to accommodate higher values.

Once the number range is corrected, try creating the delivery again and saving it — it should work as expected.

entry.title: Re: DML Perform in Crystal Report
entry.content:
You can using a Command object, there you write the SQL and CR sends it to the Server... must be valid of course. You may also be able to use a SQLExpression, it's in the Field Explorer, but.... see below...

BUT BE CAREFUL, each time the report runs it executes the Command so if you are planning to increment a counter it can cause issues if the report is not completed etc.

Also be aware if using a subreport it runs for each row of data returned in the main report.

So don't use active DB server for testing, it could causes data issue.

Ido has a lot of UFL's that can do a lot of custom things so have a chat with him so things a re clear for your work flow and data integrity...

entry.title: SBO_SP_PostTransactionNotice updates item's price but not detected by B1IF
entry.content:
Hi dear community members,


I have a question related to SAP B1 more technically:
While clients adding A/P invoice, they set sales price in the user defined field per item. After adding document my SBO_SP_PostTransactionNotice updates these items' price in the exact pricelist. I want to know what is the difference between this case and manual price change? Because SAP Customer Checkout has items synchronization job, it uses sap.POS.FetchMaterialList scenario in B1IF and 1st case it does not detect item's price changed, but if we change price manually  it detects. Which field of ITM1 I should update in procedure in order to be detected by B1IF?

Here is my procedure:

IF :object_type in ('18') AND (:transaction_type = 'A' OR :transaction_type = 'U') THEN

UPDATE "ITM1" T0
SET
"Price" = (
SELECT MAX(P."U_newPriceUZS")
FROM "PCH1" P
WHERE
P."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
AND P."DocEntry" IN ( :list_of_cols_val_tab_del )
),
"Currency" = 'UZS'
WHERE
T0."PriceList" = '5'
AND EXISTS (
SELECT 1
FROM "PCH1" P
WHERE
P."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
AND P."DocEntry" IN ( :list_of_cols_val_tab_del )
);

END IF;

SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA SAP Customer Checkout 

@Darius @elena_vavitsa @bikash_bansal

entry.title: Re: Bi weekly/Weekly Notifications for pending Time Sheet in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
@harinich -

It will work only once per week

It worked yesterday and not today because the condition in the lookup will not work today. The condition in lookup is static like -5 or -n days.

You would have set it as -5 or some number to match the start date of previous time sheet day .. this would have worked yesterday but not today 

Hence how will it work today with same -5 .. it will not right .it would work with -6 then as with each passing day that -n would change and today-n would return a different value which might not match the start date of timesheet of previous week

Hence i have clearly mentioned in the blog to run it only once per week and set that lookup condition as per your requirement .. for example if the time sheet start day is Monday , if you run the IC job on Monday every week , then use -7 .. the same -7 would not work it you run it on Tuesday (or next day or tommorrow).. Again when monday comes it will fetch the previous week time sheet with each passing week. I hope this helps

entry.title: Assistance Required – Agent-on-Fly Configuration for Logical Host (ineccdbgrp.xyz.com) in SAP Solman
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

We are currently operating an SAP ECC system on an MS-SQL environment with Always-On High Availability (HA) enabled. Our setup includes one central instance and nine additional application instances. Technical System Monitoring via SAP Solution Manager has been configured for the landscape.

Logical hostnames with dedicated IP addresses have been created on two HA nodes: ineccprdci and ineccprddb. These hosts have the Failover Cluster feature enabled and are accessible by all SAP instances. The Agent-on-Fly functionality has also been activated on both hosts.

However, we are encountering the following issues:

  • On ineccprdci, only the logical hostname ineccsapgrp.xyz.com is available for creating the Agent-on-Fly Diagnostics Agent. The logical hostname ineccdbgrp.xyz.com:1433 (used for the database listener) is not listed.

  • On ineccprddb, neither ineccsapgrp.xyz.com nor ineccdbgrp.xyz.com:1433 appear as options for Agent-on-Fly Diagnostics Agent creation.

Could you kindly advise on the correct procedure to register and configure the Agent-on-Fly Diagnostics Agent for the logical host ineccdbgrp.xyz.com:1433 on either ineccprdci or ineccprddb?

Additionally, we would appreciate your guidance on whether any configuration steps may be missing.

For your reference, below is a summary of our system landscape:

Hostname FQDN Host Type
ineccprdciineccprdci.xyz.comVirtual (MS-SQL Always-On HA & SAP Central Instance)
ineccprddbineccprddb.xyz.comVirtual (MS-SQL Always-On HA & SAP Application)
ineccsapgrpineccsapgrp.xyz.comLogical (SAP Message Server Group)
ineccdbgrp,1433ineccdbgrp.xyz.comLogical (Database Listener with Port)
infsserverinfsserver.xyz.comLogical (Shared Filesystem for SAP HA)
ineccapp2–9ineccapp[2–9].xyz.comVirtual (Application Instances)
 

We greatly appreciate your support and look forward to your guidance.

Thank you and best regards,
Srinivas V

entry.title: Issue Printing Receipts from Other POS Systems
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have successfully implemented a custom solution where receipts from multiple POS systems are retrieved from SAP Customer Checkout Manager (CCOM) and displayed on each POS using a custom button added to the Quick Selection panel.

We have also created a custom Print button that is intended to print these receipts—originally created on other POS systems—from the current POS.

Before triggering the RECEIPT_PRINT event, we set the receipt payload into the receiptModel. While the receipt displays correctly on the sales screen, attempting to print it results in the following error:
"Receipt not found".  I have Attach the Screen Shots 

 

We believe this is due to the receipt not being present in the local POS database.

How can we resolve this issue and enable printing of receipts created on other POS systems using Central Receipt Management?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

Moazzam Baig

entry.title: Re: Split costs into two GLs in one Cost center
entry.content:
Hi, 

Have you tried planning your cost via KP06 into variable and split? Usually planning using "total amount" is handled as fixed, if you want to specify an expense as variable you need to plan accordingly.

Using this method, during planned and actual activity rate calculation the system will use this distribution. This means that you may not need to use an additional cost center as you already have two specific G/L accounts.

If 400000 and 400001 are planned in KP06 as dependent on a specific activity type you might not even need to define a splitting structure. Beware that using in this way during KSS4 or KSS2 you might not find your G/L accounts as they are already allocated to the specific activity type in the variable/fixed amount.

entry.title: Re: How to covert xstring to string in sap 4.6c through create_ostream_xstring
entry.content: ABAP 4.6C is really old. You mean, how to convert xstring to string via iXML. iXML is meant to read or write XML, not to decode bytes into text. 1) If you want STRING via IXML, use CREATE_OSTREAM_CSTRING. 2) If you want to convert XSTRING to STRING, use CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE (you have to know in which code page you have your text in XSTRING, or just try your chance).

entry.title: RAP: How to Bridge UX Gap Between UUID (Backend) & Semantic Key (UI Value Help)?
entry.content:
Hi

Context/Environment:

We're developing an application using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) in a pure ABAP environment (e.g., SAP BTP ABAP Environment or a similar setup without BAS/freestyle UI5 extensions). A key customer requirement is an optimal User Experience (UX). Our database design, following SAP's recommendations for modern application development, is based on UUIDs (RAW(16)) for technical primary and foreign keys.

Problem Statement/Goal (User Experience):

While using UUIDs in the backend offers many advantages, for UI interaction, we want to present users with semantic, human-readable IDs (e.g., a Contact Name) via a Value Help.

  • The user should see a UI field (e.g., "Contact") and use it to open the Value Help.
  • After selecting an entry from the Value Help (e.g., a specific contact name), the corresponding UUID should be written to the (hidden) technical foreign key field in the entity in the background.
  • The core UX issue occurs when creating a new record, before a selection is made via the Value Help. This behavior can be observed, for instance, in the SAP Fiori Elements Feature Showcase (see also attached screenshot, e.g., FSE 007, Object Page, "Contact" field):
    • A UI field logically linked to an (unassigned or initial) UUID foreign key often initially displays the raw UUID (e.g., 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, as seen for the "Contact" field in the screenshot).
    • The Value Help itself (in the screenshot "Select: Contact") then correctly displays semantic values for selection (e.g., contact names of a character/string type like "Achim Picard").
    • After the user selects a semantic value from the Value Help, this semantic value (e.g., the contact name) is correctly displayed in the input field.
    • However, this initial display of a UUID in the input field sets incorrect expectations for the user regarding the field type and format. It causes confusion because, after selection, the field displays a value of a different type (character/string). From the customer's perspective, this is not a satisfactory User Experience – ideally, when creating a new record, the field should be initially empty or show a suitable placeholder for the expected semantic input, not the technical UUID.

    • Our Questions for the Community and SAP Experts:

      1. What is the recommended best practice in pure ABAP RAP to implement a Value Help for a UUID foreign key (aligning with SAP's recommended design) where the user-facing semantic input field is initially completely empty (and not showing a 0000...-UUID) when creating a new record, until a value is selected?
      2. How does SAP itself address this UX requirement in its own standard Fiori apps (built with RAP) or in more detailed examples like the referenced "fiori-elements-feature-showcase," specifically for fields representing UUID-based foreign keys that should be initially empty on new record creation?

      We would appreciate any pointers, best practices, or solutions that can help us optimize the User Experience for our customers within the pure ABAP RAP stack.

      Thanks in advance!

      Fouad

entry.title: Re: Account Based COPA and cost element assignments
entry.content:
Hi,

In accounting base (or margin analysis) cost elements are assigned to an Account Assignment Type. G/L Accounts, opened as cost elements, are registered in COPA with different methods i.e. OKB9, COPA assessment, settlement from internal orders or wbs.

When with costing base you would use value field to collects your quantities or amounts, now is the account itself that gathers amounts and quantity informations.

As the topic is quite vast I would recommend to make a visit at SAP Help Portal for further information. Here is a direct link on the topic: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/5e23dc8fe9be4fd496f8ab556667ea05/4cadc254440a713be10000000a44176d.html

entry.title: 7 Groundbreaking AI Announcements at SAP Sapphire 2025 You Shouldn’t Miss
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire 2025, the spotlight was firmly on Artificial Intelligence — not as a future ambition, but as the new operational core of enterprise software. SAP unveiled a suite of innovations designed to make AI tangible, scalable, and deeply integrated into business processes.

Here’s a quick rundown of the 7 most exciting AI announcements you need to know.

1. Joule Is Now Omnipresent

SAP’s AI copilot, Joule, has evolved. It’s now context-aware, ever-present, and embedded across both SAP and non-SAP tools. With its new Action Bar, Joule can proactively recommend actions and automate tasks based on user behavior. It’s your AI assistant — wherever you work.

The biggest upgrade? A Perplexity AI partnership, allowing Joule to bring in real-time, verified insights from the public web to enhance its enterprise answers.

2. Agentic AI Goes Mainstream

SAP is rolling out an ecosystem of pre-built AI agents. These aren’t just tools — they’re task-oriented mini AIs built to manage workflows across HR, supply chain, finance, and sales. Examples include:

  • Shop Floor Supervisor Agent

  • Expense Validation Agent

  • Quote Creation & Catalog Optimization Agents

They can now even collaborate across systems, thanks to the new Agent2Agent protocol developed with Google.

3. AI Agent Hub + LeanIX Integration

Managing AI agents just got easier with the new AI Agent Hub and its connection to SAP LeanIX. This lets enterprises map each agent to a specific business process or capability — bringing structure and governance to your AI operations.

4. SAP AI Foundation Becomes the OS for AI

The newly launched SAP AI Foundation is like an operating system for enterprise AI. It includes:

  • AI Agent Runtime

  • Prompt Optimizer (make your prompts smarter & model-agnostic)

  • Joule Studio for agent and skill building

  • AI Evaluation Services

  • Enterprise-wide Knowledge Graphs

This foundation gives developers and IT teams the infrastructure to build, deploy, and scale AI applications confidently.

5. Business Data Cloud Gets Smarter

SAP’s Business Data Cloud isn’t just for analytics anymore. It’s now fueling intelligent applications like:

  • People Intelligence (HR insights from SuccessFactors)

  • Spend Control Tower

  • Green Ledger for sustainability tracking

With connections to Google BigQuery, AWS, and Azure, your data is ready for intelligent automation at scale.

6. Developer-Centric AI Enhancements

If you’re building on SAP, you’re in for a treat:

  • Low-code AI agent development via Joule Studio

  • AI-powered ABAP tooling for ECC migration

  • Custom LLM integration in the Integration Suite

  • Marketplace availability via AWS and GCP

SAP’s message is clear: AI should be easy to build and run, not a black box.

7. Real-Time Intelligence with Perplexity AI

One of the most intriguing partnerships is with Perplexity AI, giving SAP users access to real-time, web-validated information — integrated directly into their business queries.

This means you’re no longer limited to internal data. Joule can now combine enterprise knowledge with trusted external content for complete, contextual answers.

Final Thoughts

SAP Sapphire 2025 marked a pivotal shift — from talking about AI to operationalizing it at scale. Whether you’re in tech, operations, or strategy, these announcements reflect one reality: AI is now core to SAP — and core to the future of enterprise transformation.

entry.title: ✨ How to Identify SAP Fiori Apps Based on RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model)
entry.content:
📚Introduction

With the rise of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP, the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) has become the gold standard for building cloud-ready, upgrade-safe applications. RAP brings together modern CDS views, OData V4 services, business object behavior definitions, and service bindings to simplify and accelerate application development.

As SAP increasingly delivers new Fiori apps using RAP—and updates existing ones into RAP-based versions—it's becoming critical for developers, architects, and even functional consultants to:

  • Identify which apps use RAP
  • Understand how to extend them using side-by-side or in-app extensibility
  • Recognize RAP-based capabilities like draft handling, validations, actions, and more

But here's the challenge: ❌SAP doesn't explicitly label Fiori apps as "RAP-based" in the Fiori Apps Library. So how can we reliably identify which apps are built using RAP?

This blog shows you how. Let's break it down with examples, tools, screenshots, and common pitfalls.

🔍Where to Begin? Fiori Apps Reference Library

Head to the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library. This is your primary tool for:

  • Searching apps by Line of Business (e.g., Finance, Procurement)
  • Filtering by SAP S/4HANA Version, UI Technology, and more
  • Getting details like OData service, App ID, and Extensibility options

⚠️But here's the catch: The library doesn't let you filter directly for "RAP-based apps". So you have to be smart about indirect clues.

 

 

 

 

 

⚡RAP Indicators in the Library

Apply these filters and clues:

  • UI Technology: Select SAP Fiori elements (RAP supports this by default)
  • Application Type: Select Transactional
  • OData Version: If available, filter or check for OData V4 (RAP requires this)
  • App Name or Version: Look for apps with names like:
    • "Manage Sales Quotation - Version 2"
    • "Manage Sales Contract - Version 2"

🔮Confirming RAP in the Backend (Step-by-Step)

If you've found a promising app in the Fiori Library, verify it in the backend using ADT (ABAP Development Tools) or SAP GUI.

✅Checklist: Is It RAP?

CheckpointRAP IndicatorTool

Service Binding exists

✅Required

ADT (Eclipse/BAS)

Service Definition used

✅Required

ADT

Behavior Definition present

✅Required

ADT

No SEGW project involved

✅Good sign

ADT or GUI

OData Version = 4.0

✅Strong hint

/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

✅RAP-Based App Example

App ID F5630 (Manage Sales Quotation - Version 2):

  • OData = V4 ✅
  • Has Service Definition: UI_SALESQUOTATIONMANAGE✅
  • Has Service Binding: UI_SALESQUOTATIONMANAGE ✅
  • Has Behavior Definition: I_SalesQuotationTP ✅

🎉Conclusion: This is a RAP-based Fiori app.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

📊Diagram: How RAP-Based Fiori App Is Structured

UI_SALESQUOTATIONMANAGE (Service Binding, OData V4)

└── UI_SALESQUOTATIONMANAGE (Service Definition)

    └── [Projection CDS View – C_SalesQuotationManageTP]

        └── I_SalesQuotationTP (Root CDS View with Behavior)

            └── Behavior Definition (BDD)

✨Conclusion

Identifying whether a Fiori app is RAP-based takes a bit of detective work, but once you understand the key indicators—OData V4, service binding, behavior definition—you'll be able to:

  • Build smarter extensions
  • Create upgrade-stable extensions
  • Use modern features like drafts, validations, and late numbering

🚀Whether you're a developer or an architect planning your SAP S/4HANA roadmap, knowing how to filter and confirm RAP apps helps you take full advantage of the modern ABAP programming model.

If you found this blog helpful, share it with your team and Happy Learning Together !! 😉

entry.title: Re: Unable to ceate new user in the SAP HANA database exp...
entry.content:
This error message: scss Copy Edit (dberror) [258]: insufficient privilege means your current user doesn’t have the required system privilege to create new users in SAP HANA Cloud. Even if you’ve assigned the development roles mentioned in the tutorial, you still need the USER ADMIN privilege or must execute the SQL as a DBADMIN or equivalent high-privileged user.

Ask your system administrator to grant the USER ADMIN related role to your user

Look for some Standard Roles:

sap.hana.xs.admin.roles::Administrator, 
sap.hana.ide.roles::SecurityAdmin

entry.title: Re: how to access to the additional data provided by an o...
entry.content:
Hi @user116 ,

Please find below steps to access the additional step.

1.Access the Onboarding Dashboard (New)

  • Navigate to Admin Center.
  • Go to Onboarding Dashboard (New).
  • Search for the specific new hire.
  • Click on Actions and select Onboarding Documents.

This will display all documents associated with the onboarding process, including those uploaded during the Additional Data Collection step.

2. Ensure Proper Role-Based Permissions (RBP)

  • To view or manage the additional data, ensure that the appropriate permissions are granted
  • Onboarding Data Collection Permissions:
  • Navigate to Admin Center > Permission Roles.
  • Select the relevant role (e.g., External Onboarding User).
  • Under Permissions > Onboarding or Offboarding Object Permission, ensure that View and Edit permissions are granted for:

3.Onboarding data collection configuration

  • Onboarding data collection user configuration
  • Visibility Settings for Custom MDF Objects:
  • Go to Admin Center > Configure Object Definition.
  • Search for the custom data collection object.
  • Ensure that the mdfSystemExternalUserVisibility is set to Read only or Editable.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Canada Compliance forms for remote employee's Province
entry.content:
Hi @Raj_SF ,

I common challenge when implementing SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0 for a multi-provincial hiring model with a single legal entity. Here's how you can address the issue both functionally and in terms of legal compliance.

Key Objective
Ensure that tax forms and deduction logic reflect the employee's home province, not just the legal entity’s location.

Current Issue
ONB 2.0 by default ties tax form generation to the legal entity (company location).

Since your only legal entity is in BC, it's triggering BC-specific forms for all new hires — which is incorrect for those living in other provinces (e.g., Quebec).

Is This Legally Compliant?
No, it is not compliant to apply the tax forms of British Columbia to employees working and residing in other provinces, such as Quebec. In Canada:

Provincial tax forms (TD1) are based on the employee's primary work or home location, not the legal entity’s location.

For example, a Quebec resident must complete TP-1015.3-V (Revenu Québec) and the federal TD1 form — not the BC provincial TD1.

Workaround/Configuration Options in SuccessFactors
Yes, there are alternatives and configuration changes that can help you achieve compliance:

1. Configure Location-Based Logic via Business Rules
Use business rules during the ONB process to derive the province of employment (based on the candidate’s home or work address) and dynamically assign correct forms:

Create a custom MDF object or field to capture "Province of Residence" or "Province of Employment".

Use that field to drive form selection via a business rule.

This logic must be set in the Data Collection Step of ONB 2.0.

Example:
If Province of Employment = Quebec, trigger:

TD1 Federal

TP1015.3-V (Quebec Provincial)

2. Update Form Mapping via ONB Configuration
You can customize which tax forms are shown based on a lookup table or business rule that references the province value captured during onboarding.

This might include:

Creating different form groups for each province.

Using form trigger rules based on the selected province.

3. Custom Panels for Province-Specific TD1 Forms
Build province-specific panels for tax forms and surface them using the rules above. For example:

Panel A: Federal + BC TD1

Panel B: Federal + Quebec TP1015

This allows you to dynamically show correct forms based on user input.

4. Job Information Sync with EC (If EC is enabled)
If you're using Employee Central, ensure the location/work address reflects the correct province to align taxation post-onboarding.

Summary:

ActionDescription
Define "Province of Employment" fieldAdd this to onboarding or pre-hire data collection
Use business rulesDynamically assign forms based on province
Validate complianceMake sure legal forms (TD1 + provincial) are properly assigned
Test for edge casesQuebec has unique needs (language, form differences)

Hope this helps!!

Please let me know if you need any help on design the business rule or a sample data model for this logic?

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Data Migration Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
What is the Data Migration Cockpit?

The Data Migration Cockpit is an SAP Fiori-based application designed to simplify and manage the migration of both master data and transactional data into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud during system implementation.

It provides a structured, user-friendly interface with the following key capabilities:

  • Predefined Migration Objects – such as Profit center, cost center, Customer, Vendor, G/L Accounts, Asset Master, etc.

  • SAP-Provided Excel Templates – for easy data input and mapping

  • Built-in Validation & Error Checking – helps identify and correct data issues before posting

  • Step-by-Step Guided Process – ensures consistent and accurate data migration

With the Data Migration Cockpit, businesses can reduce complexity, minimize errors, and ensure a smooth go-live by efficiently uploading clean legacy data into the SAP cloud system.

Steps to Perform Data Migration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Step 1: Access the Data Migration App

  • Go to the Fiori Launchpad.

  • Open the app: "Migrate Your Data – Migration Cockpit".

Step 2: Create a New Migration Project

Click “Create”

Enter a name for your project eg.. profit center Migration

When you create a migration project, it is saved but not yet started. Once you begin working on it, the status changes to "In Progress." After completing the migration steps, you mark the project as "Finished" and select how long the system should retain the project data. Once that time period ends, the project is automatically removed from the list. However, the data you uploaded will remain in the system and can still be used.

Important Details Before Starting Your Migration Project

After you create the migration project, there are some key points to understand before you begin:

  • Stage-wise Data Validation: The system performs validations at different stages. You can see how many tables are involved in the migration object and how many data records (instances) will be created in the system.

  • Mapping Tasks Table: During the mapping process, you can view detailed information about your data, including any errors or successful mappings. You will also see similar reports during the simulation and actual migration steps.

  1. Download the Template
    Get the Excel template provided by SAP for the migration object you want to upload.

  2. Prepare the Data
    Fill the template carefully with your legacy data, following the format exactly.

  3. Upload the Prepared Template
    Upload your completed Excel file into the Data Migration Cockpit.

  4. Mapping Tasks
    Review and adjust how your data fields map to SAP system fields if needed.

  5. Simulation
    Run a test migration to check for errors without actually posting data.

  6. Migration
    Execute the real data upload into SAP once the simulation is successful.

  7. Correction File
    If errors appear, download the correction file, fix the data, and repeat the upload process.

These are the steps in the migration process. After you finish one step, the system will take you to the next step automatically. You just need to follow and complete each step.

Download Template and Upload Data

Get the template as an XML file, enter the necessary data, and submit the filled template by uploading it back.

Mapping Task

The Mapping Task helps you connect the data from your uploaded file to the correct values in SAP S/4HANA.

For example:

  • You may need to match company codes, currencies, or country codes from your old system to the values used in SAP.

During this step:

  • The system shows you any missing or incorrect values.

  • You can manually adjust or confirm the correct mappings.

  • Once everything is mapped correctly, you can continue to the next step.

Simulation

The Simulation step lets you test the data migration without actually posting the data into the SAP system.

What happens during simulation:

  • The system checks if the data is correct and complete.

  • It shows any errors or warnings in the data.

  • You can review the results and fix any problems before doing the actual migration.

This step helps you avoid mistakes during the real migration.

Migration

After a successful simulation, you can start the Migration step.

In this step:

  • The system uploads the data from the staging tables into SAP S/4HANA.

  • All valid records are posted to the system.

  • You can see a summary of successful and failed records.

If there are any errors, you can fix them and repeat the migration for those records.

This is the final step where your data becomes active in SAP.

Migration Result

After the migration is complete, you can view the Migration Results to check the status of your data upload.

In this section, you will see:

  • Successful Records – These records were uploaded and posted correctly into SAP.

  • Failed Records – These records had errors and were not posted.

For failed records:

  • The system shows error messages to help you understand what went wrong.

  • You can download a Correction File, fix the issues, and re-upload the corrected data.

Reviewing the migration result helps ensure that all your data has been properly transferred into SAP S/4HANA.

The uploaded template file must be less than 100 MB in size. If your data is too large, consider splitting it into multiple files.

 

#S/4HANA Public Cloud  
#Data Migration Cockpit

entry.title: Document Reporting Compliance Central America & Latin America
entry.content:
The Central American (CAC) region is rapidly advancing its digital transformation agenda, and electronic invoicing (e-invoicing) is at the center of this evolution. Governments across the region are mandating e-invoicing for B2G, B2B, and B2C transactions, with each country introducing unique regulatory and technical requirements.

This blog post outlines the current e-invoicing mandates in the CAC region and introduces how Crimson Technology Partners supports SAP customers with a powerful SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) extension, specifically designed for the region.

 

The Regulatory Landscape Across the CAC Region

Countries such as Costa Rica, Panama, Guatemala, El Salvador, Dominican Republic are rolling out phased or full-scale e-invoicing mandates.

For instance:

  • Costa Rica: One of the earliest adopters, with universal e-invoicing required since 2019 and new regulations mandating version 4.4 implementation by September 1, 2025.
  • Panama: Enforces mandatory use of e-invoicing for both public and private sectors via the SFEP system and qualified PAC providers.
  • Dominican Republic: Law No. 32-23 mandates phased implementation through 2026 across all business segments.
  • Guatemala: Uses the FEL system with mandatory e-invoicing since 2022 for all VAT-registered taxpayers.
  • El Salvador: Requires full e-invoicing compliance by July 1, 2024.

Expanding Across Latin America: Key E-Invoicing Mandates

Beyond the Central American, Latin America continues to lead globally in electronic invoicing maturity. Countries like Mexico, Brazil, Peru, Colombia, and Chile have long-standing frameworks in place, while others like Bolivia, Paraguay, and Ecuador are advancing with phased rollouts. Here’s a look at key developments:

  • Mexico: A pioneer in e-invoicing with the CFDI (Comprobante Fiscal Digital por Internet) model. All taxpayers must issue electronic invoices validated by the SAT (Tax Administration Service) through authorized PACs. CFDI 4.0 is now the active version with added validations and stricter controls.
  • Colombia: E-invoicing is mandatory for B2G, B2B, and B2C. Invoices must include a Unique Code (CUFE) and undergo pre-validation by DIAN. Recent mandates also include electronic POS tickets phased in during 2024.
  • Peru: (Regulatory Change Manager Peru)Mandated e-invoicing for all taxpayer categories by June 2022. Operates on a pre-clearance model using the Electronic Issuance System (SEE).
  • Bolivia: Following a phased implementation by sector, mandatory for all sectors by 2025. Uses UBL 2.1 format and digital signatures validated by SIN.
  • Ecuador: Enforces mandatory e-invoicing for all transaction types, including non-resident taxpayers. Validation must occur within 24 hours through SRI (Internal Revenue Service).
  • Paraguay: Government contracts began requiring e-invoicing in 2023. Full B2B/B2C rollout spans 2025 to 2026, using the SIFEN platform.

Crimson’s CAC & Latin America Extension for SAP Document Reporting Compliance

As early adopters of the SAP solutions the Crimson team have extensive experience in Central & Latin America and their extensibility solution is designed to cover countries where there is no SAP coverage, Their experts can work across both SAP full solutions such as Mexico and Brazil and using extensibility coverage give a single tool across the region.

Key Benefits :

  • Legal UBL format in Document Reporting Compliance
    • Leveraging our CAC solution eradicates the necessity for individual country technical upgrades
  • Single strategic integration to our network
  • Synchronous responses back to SAP from the Tax Authority
  • Receiving of e-invoices in XML format (Costa Rica)
    • XMLs can be integrated to VIM or via API to other Vendor software
    • HTML view of the xml

Key Capabilities:

  • Generation of compliant electronic invoices (XML) for B2G, B2B, and B2C transactions
  • Supports SD, FI, FICA, and RE-FX billing scenarios
  • Self-billing, correction, and manual invoices supported
  • Automated or manual submission to tax authorities
  • Inbound invoice receipt and validation
  • PDF conversion and HTML invoice rendering
  • API integration with external systems or vendor solutions

Unified Network Architecture

This design ensures:

  • Real-time synchronous validation responses
  • No need for country-specific updates
  • All legal formats stored and maintained within SAP Document Reporting Compliance
  • QR code support for customer-facing documents

Real-World Example: Costa Rica

 

Crimson’s Costa Rica solution demonstrates the full power of the CAC extension:

  • Compliant XML generation for SD, FI, and FICA invoices
  • Integration with the Costa Rican Ministry of Finance
  • Automated response file processing
  • Inbound e-invoicing capabilities

Why Choose Crimson’s DRC CAC Extension?

  • Scalable across multiple countries with a single integration
  • No need for localized upgrades for each country
  • Support for inbound and outbound invoice compliance
  • Expertise in statutory reporting, tax compliance, and SAP architecture

About Crimson Technology Partners

Crimson’s SAP consultants have delivered successful implementations across 60+ countries. With offices in Liverpool, Bangalore & Lviv they bring deep global experience combined with local know-how.

Their partnership with Bluestonex enables a robust BTP-managed service offering, covering all SAP Document Reporting Compliance Solutions plus 30+ extensible solutions globally as well Master Data Orchestration for Tax & International Trade Compliance.

Learn more at: www.crimson-partners.com

Final Thoughts

The CAC region is embracing a digital tax revolution. Crimson Technology Partners, with SAP Document Reporting Compliance and its CAC Extension, empowers SAP customers to meet these compliance challenges with confidence, agility, and long-term scalability.

entry.title: Create a custom action to download an excel file with CAP
entry.content:
## **Step 1: Define Your Database Model**

We’ll use the classic `Books` model provided in the CAP [bookshop sample app](https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/sample/).

Create your `db/data-model.cds`:

namespace my.bookshop;

entity Books {
  key ID    : UUID;
  title     : String(111);
  author    : String(80);
  stock     : Integer;
  price     : Decimal(9,2);
}

Run `cds deploy` to push the model to your database.

## **Step 2: Setup the Service CDS**

Next, define your service and import the database model. We’ll also define a custom return type—this will allow us to return a file of `LargeBinary` type with an appropriate media type for Excel.

Create `srv/book-service.cds`:

using { my.bookshop as my } from '../db/data-model';

service BookService {
  // Define a type for the Excel file response
  type ExcelFile : LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet';

  // Our download action - takes a book name query and returns a file
  action downloadBooksExcel(
    searchString: String
  ) returns ExcelFile;
  
  entity Books as projection on my.Books;
}

This defines the service with an action. The response includes the `@Core.MediaType` annotation for Excel files.

## **Step 3: Implement the Action Handler (and Generate Excel with exceljs)**

Let’s implement the CAP handler for this action.

npm install exceljs

**Create `srv/book-service.js`:**

const cds = require('@sap/cds')
const ExcelJS = require('exceljs')

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function() {
  const { Books } = this.entities

  this.on('downloadBooksExcel', async (req) => {
    const { searchString } = req.data

    // Filter books by title pattern
    const filters = searchString ? { title: { like: `%${searchString}%` } } : {}
    const books = await cds.run(
      SELECT.from(Books).where(filters)
    )

    // Initialize a new workbook and worksheet
    const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook()
    const worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet('Books')

    // Add columns
    worksheet.columns = [
      { header: 'ID', key: 'ID' },
      { header: 'Title', key: 'title' },
      { header: 'Author', key: 'author' },
      { header: 'Stock', key: 'stock' },
      { header: 'Price', key: 'price' }
    ]

    // Add rows
    books.forEach(book => worksheet.addRow(book))

    // Generate buffer
    const buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer()
    return buffer
  })
})

 

- We **import the db model**.

- Filter books whose title matches `searchString`.

- **Use `exceljs` to generate the Excel file in memory and return as a binary buffer.**

- Since the CDS metadata specifies the file as `LargeBinary` and assigns the correct media type, the service will automatically deliver this as a proper download.

Thank you!

entry.title: Revenue recognition to be done through Result Analysis from Deferred Revenue
entry.content:
Hi 

We have a sales contract to maintain to asset sold to the customer. For example, a machinery is sold but extended contract support for 1 year to support any issues arising out of the product.

A contract is created through VF41 with Billing plan for 1 year and billing every month by raising an invoice

The contract amounts every month to be billed to the customer 200$ 

With ref to the contract every month an invoice will be raised on the customer and FI entries are 

Dr Customer 200$ 

Cr. Def Rev 200$ (Balance sheet account)

since the billing done beginning of the month. Now for business reason they want to recognize 180$ at the end of the month

When I tried to run system recognizes the full amount 200$ which we do not want, want to recognize only 170$. We do not know where to enter the 170$.

 Deferred revenue above is a balance sheet account which we do not know   how to make this RA Key recognize the Balance Sheet account.

In the example above next month we will get another invoice 200$ but again only 160$ we want to recognize

So at the end of period deferred account will have a balance of 70$.

finally at the end of the contract we want move the entire deferred revenue bal to actual revenue say period 12 assuming this is final period of the contract.

Pl help

Thanks
Revanth
